solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int longestCommonSubsequence(String text1, String text2) {
        int m = text1.length();
        int n = text2.length();
        int[][] dp = new int[m + 1][n + 1];

        for (int i = 1; i <= m; i++) {
            for (int j = 1; j <= n; j++) {
                if (text1.charAt(i - 1) == text2.charAt(j - 1)) {
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i - 1][j - 1] + 1;
                } else {
                    dp[i][j] = Math.max(dp[i - 1][j], dp[i][j - 1]);
                }
            }
        }

        return dp[m][n];
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(m * n)
space complexity: O(m * n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution uses dynamic programming to build a 2D array `dp` where `dp[i][j]` represents the length of the longest common subsequence of the substrings `text1[0..i-1]` and `text2[0..j-1]`. The algorithm iterates through both strings, comparing characters and updating the `dp` table accordingly. The key strength is its optimal time complexity for the problem size, while the main weakness is the use of O(m * n) space, which could be optimized further with a space-efficient approach.
